message:
chore(beads): close four review findings resolved in prior fixes

Mark four bead entries as closed after their underlying fixes landed.
No status regressions, no new issues, no descriptions or priorities
changed — only `status`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, and `close_reason`
moved forward for the following ids:

- flywheel_connectors-0ysa3 "[DEADLOCK] fcp-host subprocess RPC holds
  runner mutex across unbounded child I/O" — closed with
  "Phantom JSONL/DB race — bead was closed in session; re-close to
  reconcile state". Mitigation for the mutex-across-I/O shipped
  earlier; this entry was re-opened by a stale JSONL merge and is now
  being reconciled.

- flywheel_connectors-8t034 "[REVIEW-CONVERGE][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap
  certificate chain accepts expired certs" — closed with
  "Rejected expired and not-yet-valid X.509 provisioning chains
  during certificate selection". Fix is commit 9a161717
  (fix(fcp-bootstrap): enforce certificate validity windows).

- flywheel_connectors-9zrs2 "[REVIEW-CONVERGE][LOW] fcp-bootstrap
  soft-token shibboleth prefix bypasses crypto in production code" —
  closed with "Removed the soft-token DER prefix bypass so bootstrap
  always enforces X.509 issuer verification". Fix is commit 176c0115
  (9zrs2 require crypto validation for soft-token certs).

- flywheel_connectors-mostw "[REVIEW-CONVERGE][HIGH] fcp-host
  dispatcher can misroute late subprocess replies after timeout" —
  closed with "fcp-host subprocess RPC now generates and validates
  per-request JSON-RPC ids (and tracks an epoch / poison flag)". Fix
  is the preceding commit in this push
  (fix(fcp-host): validate JSON-RPC response ids + poison transport
  on I/O error).

The JSONL is the source of truth for local beads state and is pushed
up so collaborating agents see the closures without having to re-run
the corresponding reviews.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0s6","title":"fcp.docusign: Add Recipients and Fields","description":"# fcp.docusign: Add Recipients and Fields (FCP2)

## Goal
Configure envelope recipients and signature fields (tabs) in a safe, typed way.

## Scope
- Add recipients (signer/CC/etc) with routing order.
- Add tabs/fields:
  - signature
  - initials
  - date signed
  - text fields
  - checkbox
- Support both:
  - coordinate-based placement
  - anchor-tag placement (preferred when possible)

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.envelopes.write`
- `docusign.envelopes.tabs.write`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - recipient emails/names are sensitive; never log
- Deterministic field placement:
  - stable IDs for recipient roles/tabs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.envelope.add_recipient`
  - `docusign.envelope.update_recipient`
  - `docusign.envelope.add_tabs`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - recipient routing rules
  - anchor-tag placement
  - redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Envelopes can be configured reproducibly without leaking recipient PII.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-10r4k","title":"FIX: SymbolResponseBuilder was_bounded false positive when symbols exactly equal limit","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:38.754934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49.337106Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49.337094Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mesh"],"comments":[{"id":733,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-10r4k","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Root cause: add_from_repair_engine() used `available_count >= max_response_symbols` to detect bounding, but `select_symbols()` already caps its return at `max_response_symbols`, making `available_count == max_response_symbols` trigger even when all available symbols were returned.

Fix: Moved was_bounded computation to `build()` where `total_available` (the true object store count) is known. Now `was_bounded = sent_count < remaining` using the actual counts rather than relying on the post-selection count from the engine.

All 508 mesh tests pass (425 unit + 81 integration + 2 doc). The previously-failing `meshnode_symbol_request_is_final_when_all_sent` now passes, and the existing `response_builder_marks_bounded_when_request_limit_hits` continues to pass.","created_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-11g2","title":"E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling","description":"# E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling

## Goal
Define a single structured logging schema for E2E scripts and fcp-e2e harness output, plus a validator.

## Scope
- JSONL schema (fields: timestamp, script, step, correlation_id, duration_ms, result, artifacts, error_code, details).
- Validator tool or test that fails if logs are malformed.
- Documented guidelines for step logging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is documented under docs/testing/.
- Validator runs in CI or as part of e2e runner.
- All existing e2e scripts conform to the schema.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","title":"fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions","description":"# fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose a tightly-controlled moderation surface for moderators:
- approve/remove content
- manage mod queue
- ban/mute users (optional)

These actions are high-blast-radius and must be policy + approval gated.

## Scope
- List modqueue/reports.
- Approve/remove a post/comment.
- Optional: ban user (requires strict policy).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.mod.read`
- `reddit.mod.write` (dangerous)
- Optional: `reddit.mod.ban` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For any moderation write:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - receipts + audit events include thing_id + action + reason (sanitized)
- Avoid logging raw user content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.mod.queue.list`
  - `reddit.mod.approve`
  - `reddit.mod.remove`
  - Optional: `reddit.mod.ban_user`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - forbidden/permission errors

## Acceptance criteria
- Moderation actions are mechanically authorized and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","title":"[MOR/C1.1] Implement revocation check-use atomicity via RevocationSeal pattern","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:31:01.347311Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:46.041964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:46.041202Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","revocation","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:41.418240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:39.593999Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2627,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Implement a RevocationSeal type returned by RevocationRegistry::check_with_seal() that binds the revocation check result to a specific revocation_head_seq at check time. The seal must be re-validated at operation commit time. If the seal is stale (head_seq advanced since check), the operation must be re-checked or aborted. WHY: H2 Adversarial found a TOCTOU vulnerability ranked #1 CRITICAL: revocation check passes at T1, revocation is inserted at T2, operation executes at T3 against a revoked token. The seal pattern converts this into: check at T1 returns seal(head_seq=N), operation executes, at commit time seal is re-validated against current head_seq. If head_seq advanced, re-check or abort. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs — add RevocationSeal struct { checked_at: u64, head_seq: u64, token_id: ObjectId, decision: RevocationDecision }. Add check_with_seal() to RevocationRegistry trait. Add validate_seal() that compares seal.head_seq against current frontier. Require CapabilityVerified to carry the seal. ACCEPTANCE: (1) RevocationSeal type exists with checked_at, head_seq, token_id, decision. (2) check_with_seal() returns RevocationSeal alongside the decision. (3) validate_seal() rejects stale seals when head_seq has advanced. (4) CapabilityVerified carries revocation_seal field. (5) At least 5 tests: fresh seal passes, stale seal triggers re-check, concurrent revocation detected, seal with wrong token_id rejected, seal serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days. DESIGN NOTE: The seal is NOT a lock — it is an optimistic concurrency control mechanism. Most operations will find the seal still valid. Only operations that race with a revocation event will need re-checking. This keeps the happy path fast while closing the TOCTOU gap.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:26Z"},{"id":2647,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C3.1 (phantom tokens) because RevocationSeal is carried inside CapabilityVerified, which is the result of CapabilityToken<Verified>::verify(). The seal pattern and phantom token pattern must be designed together so that verify() returns both the Verified token AND the revocation seal in one atomic step.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:40Z"},{"id":2671,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","author":"jemanuel","text":"C1.1 done: RevocationSeal, RevocationDecision, SealValidation types + check_with_seal/validate_seal on RevocationRegistry. 6 acceptance tests, clippy clean. Commit 73832f34.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:28:45Z"}]}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8t034","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap certificate chain accepts expired certs (no validity period check)","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1240-1270 (commit 1889e2df br-jx3r4) certificate_has_x509_issuer_chain verifies ONLY the cryptographic signature via x509_parser verify_signature. It does NOT check the validity period (not_before / not_after) or any revocation signal (CRL/OCSP). A provisioning certificate whose not_after has passed still chains cleanly and select_certificate_for_provisioning returns it as the winning candidate. For a bootstrap flow that mints owner identity, this means a long-decommissioned identity can still drive genesis creation until the token is physically retired. Suggested fix: after verify_signature, also check that tbs.validity.not_before <= now and tbs.validity.not_after > now (a clock injection seam already exists elsewhere in fcp-bootstrap for deterministic tests). Also consider leaf-only EKU check for code-signing / key-cert-sign depending on what the provisioning certificate is used for.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8tv","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation)","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation) (FCP2)

## Goal
Define a safe-by-default zone strategy for Kubernetes operations, especially production.

Important: the connector instance is **single-zone bound**. “Dev/staging/prod isolation” is achieved by deploying separate connector instances bound to separate zones and pinned to separate cluster API hosts.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Scope
- Recommended zone naming patterns (policy-owned):
  - `z:work:k8s:dev:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:staging:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:prod:<cluster>`
- Capability tiering guidance:
  - readonly vs deploy vs exec
  - forbid deletes in prod by default
- Approval guidance:
  - prod deploy and exec should require ApprovalToken

## Deliverables
- Documented default policy expectations (reason codes + deny-by-default rules) referenced by connector operations.
- Clear mapping between zone policy and Kubernetes RBAC contexts.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Policy-oriented tests (mock-only) verifying:
  - prod zone denies mutations by default
  - approval gating required for exec/deploy

## Acceptance criteria
- A user can set up three connector instances and be mechanically confident prod is protected.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8v34","title":"TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests (DeviceEnrollment, NodeKeyAttestation, Removal Workflow)","description":"# TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that device enrollment and removal work correctly and securely.

## Test Categories

### DeviceEnrollment Object
- [ ] Valid enrollment parses correctly
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Tailscale node_id binding correct
- [ ] Authorized tags match
- [ ] Expiry (if present) enforced
- [ ] Malformed enrollment rejected

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node_id to pubkeys
- [ ] signing_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] encryption_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] issuance_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] KID mappings correct
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Attestation expiry enforced

### Enrollment Flow
- [ ] New node can enroll with valid enrollment object
- [ ] Enrolled node receives zone keys (ZoneKeyManifest)
- [ ] Enrolled node can participate in sessions
- [ ] Enrolled node appears in peer discovery

### Key Rotation
- [ ] Node can rotate its keys
- [ ] New attestation replaces old
- [ ] Old KIDs become invalid
- [ ] Sessions using old keys gracefully terminated

### Device Removal
- [ ] Removal revokes enrollment object
- [ ] Removal revokes attestation
- [ ] Removal revokes issuer keys
- [ ] Tokens from removed node rejected
- [ ] Zone keys rotated after removal
- [ ] Removed node cannot participate

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Replay old enrollment: rejected (revoked or expired)
- [ ] Forge enrollment: invalid owner signature rejected
- [ ] Use removed node keys: rejected after revocation propagates
- [ ] Impersonate node_id: attestation signature check fails

## Golden Vectors
- Valid DeviceEnrollment bytes
- Valid NodeKeyAttestation bytes
- Removal workflow sequence

## Integration Tests
- [ ] Full enrollment → operation → removal cycle
- [ ] Multi-node enrollment
- [ ] Concurrent enrollment attempts

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "node_id": "...",
  "operation": "enroll|attest|rotate|remove",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Enrollment flow works end-to-end
- Removal flow invalidates all node authority
- No stale credentials accepted after removal

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8w4","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a controlled exec surface for debugging:
- run a constrained command in a container

Exec is **high risk** (arbitrary code execution in prod). Must be approval gated and heavily audited.

## Scope
- Start an exec session with:
  - explicit namespace/pod/container
  - explicit command allowlist (policy-owned)
  - explicit timeout
- Capture session transcript metadata (not raw output by default).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.exec` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Approval gating recommended default for exec.
- Audit:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent including command hash + targets
  - never log secrets/output by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.exec.start`
  - `k8s.exec.status`
  - `k8s.exec.cancel`
- Safety layer:
  - deny interactive shells by default
  - deny dangerous commands unless explicitly allowed

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - allowlist enforcement
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Exec cannot run without explicit authorization and is fully auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9ut","title":"fcp.arxiv: Citation graph traversal (cited-by and references)","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Citation graph traversal (cited-by and references) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide citation graph access to support literature review workflows:
- references (outgoing)
- cited-by (incoming)
- small bounded subgraph traversal

## Data source constraints
arXiv’s API does **not** provide complete citation data. This bead supports two strategies:
1. **Preferred**: query an external citation index (e.g., Semantic Scholar) when policy enables it.
2. **Fallback**: parse references from PDF/LaTeX (best-effort, lower accuracy).

## Scope
- Get references for a paper.
- Get cited-by for a paper (preferred via external index).
- Build bounded citation subgraph:
  - max depth
  - max nodes

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.citations.read`
- `semanticscholar.citations.read` (optional dependency)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to whichever source is enabled.
- Output is bounded and deterministic.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.citations.references`
  - `arxiv.citations.cited_by`
  - `arxiv.citations.subgraph`
- Canonical output type:
  - `CitationGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - graph bounds enforcement
  - deterministic traversal ordering
  - error mapping when citation source unavailable

## Acceptance criteria
- Citation graph traversal is useful, bounded, and policy-controlled.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-9zrs2","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][LOW] fcp-bootstrap soft-token shibboleth prefix bypasses crypto in production code","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1196-1235 (commit 1889e2df br-jx3r4) SOFT_TOKEN_CERT_PREFIX = b"SOFT-CERT-V1:" is an ASCII magic-byte sentinel in production code (not gated by cfg(test) or a test-mocks feature). When a certificates DER bytes start with this prefix, certificate_has_verified_issuer_chain_inner routes through certificate_has_soft_token_issuer_chain which does NO cryptographic verification; it only checks subject/issuer string equality. Real X.509 DER starts with 0x30 0x82 (SEQUENCE tag) so theres no collision today, but a token that stores a non-X.509 blob whose first 13 bytes happen to match the ASCII prefix would pass the chain walk with zero crypto. Low severity because a correctly-implemented PKCS#11 token rejects non-X.509 certificate objects at enumeration time, but the broader smell is that this path is unconditionally compiled into release builds. Suggested fix: gate certificate_has_soft_token_issuer_chain and the SOFT_TOKEN_CERT_PREFIX behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))] matching the pattern already used by MockTufVerifier / MockSigstoreVerifier in fcp-registry (br-grudr). Production builds would then never accept the shibboleth.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a0x","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core entity state access:
- read current state/attributes
- perform controlled state writes where supported

## Scope
### Read
- Get entity state by entity_id.
- Bulk list states (bounded).

### Write (domain-limited)
- For entities that support direct state writes (e.g., input_* helpers), allow setting state.
- For device control (lights/switches/etc), prefer service calls (`flywheel_connectors-3ef`).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.entities.read`
- `ha.entities.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Sensitive attributes (location, camera URLs, etc.) must be redacted from logs.
- Writes emit receipts + audit events.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.entity.get`
  - `ha.entity.list`
  - `ha.entity.set_state` (domain-limited)

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - state parsing
  - redaction rules
  - write gating
  - receipts/audit for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Entity access is reliable, bounded, and privacy-preserving.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-mostw","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][HIGH] fcp-host dispatcher can misroute late subprocess replies after timeout","description":"P2 converge finding from commit 58e25d0e. Evidence: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:194-199 creates a JSON-RPC request id, but the response path at 234-239 ignores ids entirely and accepts any next line's `result`. ConnectorProcessRunner::read_json at 814-829 applies a 10s stdout timeout and request() at 832-835 returns that timeout without draining or resynchronizing stdout. Bug scenario: request A times out in read_json, the connector emits A's response later, then request B is sent and read_json consumes A's stale response as B because no id match is checked. That can misattribute invoke/handshake/health replies across logical calls and poison the connector session after one slow RPC. Suggested fix: carry the expected request id through ConnectorRpcRequest/request(), validate the response id before accepting it, and treat any timed-out or mismatched response as a poisoned transport that must be restarted or hard-drained before issuing another RPC; simple timeout errors are not enough because JSON-RPC framing has already desynchronized.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:09:05.507240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:10:53.289963Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n6r","title":"fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval)","description":"# fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval) (FCP2)

## Goal
Make Terraform operations mechanically safe:
- plan is always read-only
- apply is always approval-gated and bound to a specific plan hash
- destroy is forbidden by default

This bead defines the safety envelope that all other terraform operations must obey.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Core safety rules (non-negotiable)
- Never run Terraform with `-auto-approve`.
- Always run with `-input=false`.
- Apply requires:
  - a previously generated plan reference
  - a matching plan hash
  - an ApprovalToken when policy requires
- Explicit denial reason codes for:
  - missing plan
  - hash mismatch
  - drift detected
  - destroy forbidden

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan`
- `terraform.apply` (dangerous; approval required)
- `terraform.destroy` (critical; disabled unless explicitly enabled)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace allowlist
  - restrict process execution to terraform binary
- Audit:
  - all state reads are audited
  - all applies produce receipts + audit events

## Deliverables
- Safety gate layer used by all terraform operations.
- Canonical DecisionReceipt reason codes for denials.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests for:
  - refusal of auto-approve
  - refusal of apply without plan
  - hash mismatch denial
  - destroy forbidden default
  - audit event emission on state reads
